A Hands-On Beginner's Guide to Mastering AI, Supervised, Unsupervised, and Deep Learning Algorithms. A Beginner's Guide to Machine Learning with Hands-on Coding and Real-World Applications
  • Covers key algorithms and techniques
  • Ideal for students and professionals
  • Hands-on implementation included

Master the fundamentals of ML and take the first step towards a career in AI!

In today's rapidly evolving world, machine learning (ML) is no longer just for researchers or data scientists. From personalized recommendations on streaming platforms to fraud detection in banking, ML powers many aspects of our daily lives. As industries increasingly adopt AI-driven solutions, learning machine learning has become a valuable skill. Yet, many find the subject overwhelming, often intimidated by its mathematical complexity. That's where Machine Learning Essentials You Always Wanted to Know (Machine Learning Essentials) comes in. This beginner-friendly guide offers a structured, step-by-step approach to understanding machine learning concepts without unnecessary jargon. Whether you are a student, a professional looking to transition into AI, or simply curious about how machines learn, this book provides a clear and practical roadmap to mastering ML.

Authored by Dhairya Parikh, an experienced data engineer who returned to academia to refine his expertise, this book bridges the gap between theory and real-world application. It simplifies the core concepts of ML, breaking them down into digestible explanations paired with hands-on coding exercises to help you apply what you learn.

What You'll Learn:

  • The fundamentals of machine learning and how it powers modern technology
  • The three key types of ML-Supervised, Unsupervised, and Reinforcement Learning
  • How to combine algorithms, data, and models to develop AI-driven solutions
  • Practical coding techniques to build and implement machine learning models

Part of Vibrant Publishers' Self-Learning Management Series, this book serves as a valuable guide for building machine learning skills, enhancing your expertise, and advancing your career in AI and data science.

Read more

About the authors

Follow authors to get new release updates, plus improved recommendations.
Previous page

  1. Vibrant Publishers

    Brief content visible, double tap to read full content.
    Full content visible, double tap to read brief content.

    Founded in 2001 by Avani and Deep Udeshi, Vibrant Publishers began with a bold mission: to make learning clear, practical, and accessible to everyone. What started as a response to complex and overwhelming educational resources has grown into a trusted publishing house with a global footprint.

    At Vibrant, our vision is to transform how the world learns. We believe that high-quality educational content should simplify learning, not complicate it. Over the years, we’ve built powerful series of books to do just that:

    Test Prep Series – Targeted resources for exams like the SAT, ACT, GRE, and GMAT, designed with authentic questions, detailed explanations, and adaptive digital tools to help students succeed.

    Self-Learning Management Series – Concise, beginner-friendly books covering key business, management, and technical topics such as HR, Marketing, Finance, Data Science, and more, ideal for career transitioners, professionals, and students.

    Job Interview Questions Series – Real-world questions and expert strategies for cracking technical and behavioral interviews in IT and business domains.

    Legends Series: A tribute to the pioneers of marketing and consumer research, our Legend in Marketing, Legend in Consumer Behavior, and Legend in Strategic Marketing series feature the collected works of globally respected scholars such as Philip Kotler, Jagdish Sheth, V. Kumar, Gerald Zaltman, Kent B. Monroe, and George S. Day, among others.

    See more on the author's page

  2. Dhairya Parikh

    Brief content visible, double tap to read full content.
    Full content visible, double tap to read brief content.

    Dhairya Parikh is a skilled data engineer with more than three years of hands-on experience in the technical consulting industry. He has a proven track record of helping clients across diverse sectors leverage data to drive strategic insights, optimize processes, and overcome complex challenges. Dhairya’s journey in data science led him to pursue a master’s degree at the University of Waterloo, where he specialized in Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence. This academic foundation, paired with his industry expertise, equips him with a unique perspective on both the theory and practical applications of cutting-edge AI and ML technologies.

    See more on the author's page

How are ratings calculated?
To calculate the overall star rating and percentage breakdown by star, we don’t use a simple average. Instead, our system considers things like how recent a review is and if the reviewer bought the item on Amazon. It also analyses reviews to verify trustworthiness.

    It's for people with no background in sciences
    Reviewed in India on 1 November 2025

Top reviews from other countries

  • Brenda James
    5 out of 5 stars
    An Incredible Guide!
    Reviewed in the United States on 8 July 2025
    Format: Kindle Edition
    Verified Purchase
    Brief content visible, double tap to read full content.
    Full content visible, double tap to read brief content.

    This book is a great introduction to the world of machine learning, especially if you're just starting out. I really appreciated how the author made what could be a daunting subject feel approachable. The explanations are clear, and he does a great job breaking down complex concepts like supervised and unsupervised learning without drowning you in math or jargon. The hands-on coding exercises were super helpful for me—being able to apply what I was reading right away made the concepts stick. I also liked how the book covers real-world uses of machine learning, like how it's used in recommendations or fraud detection. It felt relevant, not just theoretical. If you're looking to get a solid grasp of ML, whether for career reasons or just out of curiosity, this book is a perfect starting point. It’s informative, practical, and definitely not overwhelming. Highly recommend it!

    Read moreRead less

    Brief content visible, double tap to read full content.
    Full content visible, double tap to read brief content.

    As someone who’s always been curious about machine learning but slightly terrified of the math-heavy explanations (I'm just terrible at them), I found this book to be a breath of fresh air. It does exactly what it promises, it breaks down complex concepts into something that actually feels approachable, even if you’re not a data scientist or a math wizard. The real-world examples were a nice touch too. If I had one small critique, it’s that while the book is beginner-friendly, a little prior coding experience (especially in Python) definitely helps. It’s not a total dealbreaker, but absolute beginners might need to pause occasionally to look things up.

    Overall, this is a great starting point if you’re looking to dip your toes into machine learning without drowning in equations.

    The perfect starting point for anyone looking to break into the world of AI and machine learning—without getting overwhelmed by complex math or technical jargon. Whether you're a student, a professional pivoting into tech, or just curious about how machines learn, this book makes the topic approachable and engaging. Author Dhairya Parikh does an excellent job of breaking down core ML concepts—like supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning—and explaining how these ideas power the real-world technology we use every day. What makes this book stand out is its hands-on approach: you’re not just reading theory, you’re actively coding and applying what you learn.

    Helpful Book
    Reviewed in the United States on 27 July 2025
    Format: Kindle Edition
    Verified Purchase
    Brief content visible, double tap to read full content.
    Full content visible, double tap to read brief content.

    Dhairya Parikh’s Machine Learning Essentials You Always Wanted to Know is a practical and beginner-friendly introduction to machine learning. The book tackles complex concepts like supervised and unsupervised learning clearly, avoiding heavy math or jargon. I appreciated the hands-on coding exercise. There are also a ton of visuals that help learning, putting what I was trying to learn in a more understandable format. Parikh also includes links to his blog and tutorials for further study. Some algebra is college-level, but not overwhelming. Real-world examples like fraud detection and recommendation systems made the information relevant. A great starting point for just about everyone. Highly recommended.

  • Nancy Retallick, PhD
    5 out of 5 stars
    Demystifying Machine Learning
    Reviewed in the United States on 8 July 2025
    Format: Kindle Edition
    Verified Purchase
    Brief content visible, double tap to read full content.
    Full content visible, double tap to read brief content.

    As a consultant seeking to remain relevant in a rapidly evolving digital landscape, this book offers an approachable yet substantive foundation for understanding one of today’s most transformative technologies. Parikh uses a hands-on aspect of programming which demystifies machine learning for beginners. The author also includes helpful links to his blog and other online tutorials, which makes it easy to keep learning outside the book. It made me feel like I could actually start using this stuff in my consulting work, especially when dealing with data-heavy projects. Some of the algebraic content leans more toward the college level, but even then, I didn’t feel overwhelmed—just enough to give me a sense of how the math behind the models works. Overall, this book is a great starting point for consultants or professionals who want to dip their toes into machine learning without feeling lost. It helped me connect the dots between theory and practice—and that’s something I know will benefit my clients too.
